## 9121307a-3a0d-4cd4-a30a-2184ad39afec
This server helps users set up and manage their Shopify stores.

## Store Creation
Users who don't have a store yet can use get-new-store-previews to create one from a short description.
After calling get-new-store-previews, encourage the user to browse the previews and pick the theme they like best.
Each preview has a signup link — signing up through it creates a real Shopify store with that theme and matching products pre-installed.
Encourage continued brainstorming: users can try different descriptions, tweak ideas, or explore new directions without commitment.

## Store Management
Once a store exists, this server provides tools for managing it:
- Products: search, create, update, bulk-update status, and organize into collections.
- Collections: search, create, update, and manage product groupings.
- Orders: list recent orders and get detailed order information.
- Customers: list and search customers.
- Inventory: check inventory levels and set quantities at specific locations.
- Analytics: run ShopifyQL queries for sales, order, and product performance data.
- Discounts: create percentage-based discount codes.
- Images: upload images to Shopify's CDN for use in products.

## GraphQL — General-Purpose Access
The Shopify Admin API exposes hundreds of resources. The built-in tools above are optimized shortcuts for common operations (with structured output and widgets), but they only cover a subset of the API.
When the user asks about a resource that has no dedicated built-in tool — such as gift cards, metafields, metaobjects, pages, blogs, markets, translations, publications, and many others — use graphql_query (for reads) or graphql_mutation (for writes) to fulfill the request. NEVER tell the user that data is unavailable just because there is no built-in tool for it.

Decision logic:
1. If a built-in tool matches the request, prefer it (better UX).
2. If no built-in tool exists, use graphql_query or graphql_mutation.

## Shopify Developer Tools
These tools help you construct correct GraphQL operations. They do

attachment.content:
- make: Run the project Makefile (regenerates Xcode project via xcodegen and opens it).
- post-merge: Sync main after a PR has been merged (git checkout main + pull --ff-only + make).
- pr: Wrap current uncommitted changes into a Feature/RAD-XXXX branch and open a Bitbucket PR.
- deep-research: Deep research harness — fan-out web searches, fetch sources, adversarially verify claims, synthesize a cited report. - When the user wants a deep, multi-source, fact-checked research report on any topic. BEFORE invoking, check if the question is specific enough to research directly — if underspecified (e.g., "what car to buy" without budget/use-case/region), ask 2-3 clarifying questions to narrow scope. Then pass the refined question as args, weaving the answers in.
- pdf-viewer:annotate: Collaboratively annotate a PDF — propose markup, review together, iterate
- pdf-viewer:fill-form: Fill PDF form fields interactively with live visual feedback
- pdf-viewer:open: Open a PDF in the interactive viewer
- pdf-viewer:sign: Place a signature or initials image on a PDF
- anthropic-skills:consolidate-memory: Reflective pass over your memory files — merge duplicates, fix stale facts, prune the index.
- anthropic-skills:docx: Use this skill whenever the user wants to create, read, edit, or manipulate Word documents (.docx files). Triggers include: any mention of 'Word doc', 'word document', '.docx', or requests to produce professional documents with formatting like tables of contents, headings, page numbers, or letterheads. Also use when extracting or reorganizing content from .docx files, inserting or replacing images in documents, performing find-and-replace in Word files, working with tracked changes or comments, or converting content into a polished Word document. If the user asks for a 'report', 'memo', 'letter', 'template', or similar deliverable as a Word or .docx file, use this skill. Do NOT use for PDFs, spreadsheets, Google Docs, or general coding tasks unrelated to document generation.
- anthropic-skills:extract-design: Extract the full design language from any website URL. Produces 8 output files including AI-optimized markdown, visual HTML preview, Tailwind config, React theme, shadcn/ui theme, Figma variables, W3C design tokens, and CSS variables. Also runs WCAG accessibility scoring. Use when user says 'extract design', 'get design system', 'design language', 'design tokens', 'what colors/fonts does this site use', or '/extract-design'.
- anthropic-skills:pdf: Use this skill whenever the user wants to do anything with PDF files. This includes reading or extracting text/tables from PDFs, combining or merging multiple PDFs into one, splitting PDFs apart, rotating pages, adding watermarks, creating new PDFs, filling PDF forms, encrypting/decrypting PDFs, extracting images, and OCR on scanned PDFs to make them searchable. If the user mentions a .pdf file or asks to produce one, use this skill.
- anthropic-skills:pptx: Use this skill any time a .pptx file is involved in any way — as input, output, or both. This includes: creating slide decks, pitch decks, or presentations; reading, parsing, or extracting text from any .pptx file (even if the extracted content will be used elsewhere, like in an email or summary); editing, modifying, or updating existing presentations; combining or splitting slide files; working with templates, layouts, speaker notes, or comments. Trigger whenever the user mentions "deck," "slides," "presentation," or references a .pptx filename, regardless of what they plan to do with the content afterward. If a .pptx file needs to be opened, created, or touched, use this skill.
- anthropic-skills:setup-cowork: Guided Cowork setup — install role-matched plugins, connect your tools, try a skill.
- anthropic-skills:skill-creator: Create new skills, modify and improve existing skills, and measure skill performance. Use when users want to create a skill from scratch, edit, or optimize an existing skill, run evals to test a skill, benchmark skill performance with variance analysis, or optimize a skill's description for better triggering accuracy.
- anthropic-skills:xlsx: Use this skill any time a spreadsheet file is the primary input or output. This means any task where the user wants to: open, read, edit, or fix an existing .xlsx, .xlsm, .csv, or .tsv file (e.g., adding columns, computing formulas, formatting, charting, cleaning messy data); create a new spreadsheet from scratch or from other data sources; or convert between tabular file formats. Trigger especially when the user references a spreadsheet file by name or path — even casually (like "the xlsx in my downloads") — and wants something done to it or produced from it. Also trigger for cleaning or restructuring messy tabular data files (malformed rows, misplaced headers, junk data) into proper spreadsheets. The deliverable must be a spreadsheet file. Do NOT trigger when the primary deliverable is a Word document, HTML report, standalone Python script, database pipeline, or Google Sheets API integration, even if tabular data is involved.
- pdf-viewer:view-pdf: Interactive PDF viewer. Use when the user wants to open, show, or view a PDF and collaborate on it visually — annotate, highlight, stamp, fill form fields, place signature/initials, or review markup together. Not for summarization or text extraction (use native Read instead).
- update-config: Use this skill to configure the Claude Code harness via settings.json. Automated behaviors ("from now on when X", "each time X", "whenever X", "before/after X") require hooks configured in settings.json - the harness executes these, not Claude, so memory/preferences cannot fulfill them. Also use for: permissions ("allow X", "add permission", "move permission to"), env vars ("set X=Y"), hook troubleshooting, or any changes to settings.json/settings.local.json files. Examples: "allow npm commands", "add bq permission to global settings", "move permission to user settings", "set DEBUG=true", "when claude stops show X". For simple settings like theme/model, suggest the /config command.
- keybindings-help: Use when the user wants to customize keyboard shortcuts, rebind keys, add chord bindings, or modify ~/.claude/keybindings.json. Examples: "rebind ctrl+s", "add a chord shortcut", "change the submit key", "customize keybindings".
- verify: Verify that a code change actually does what it's supposed to by running the app and observing behavior. Use when asked to verify a PR, confirm a fix works, test a change manually, check that a feature works, or validate local changes before pushing.
- code-review: Review the current diff for correctness bugs and reuse/simplification/efficiency cleanups at the given effort level (low/medium: fewer, high-confidence findings; high→max: broader coverage, may include uncertain findings). Pass --comment to post findings as inline PR comments, or --fix to apply the findings to the working tree after the review.
- simplify: Review the changed code for reuse, simplification, efficiency, and altitude cleanups, then apply the fixes. Quality only — it does not hunt for bugs; use /code-review for that.
- fewer-permission-prompts: Scan your transcripts for common read-only Bash and MCP tool calls, then add a prioritized allowlist to project .claude/settings.json to reduce permission prompts.
- loop: Run a prompt or slash command on a recurring interval (e.g. /loop 5m /foo). Omit the interval to let the model self-pace. - When the user wants to set up a recurring task, poll for status, or run something repeatedly on an interval (e.g. "check the deploy every 5 minutes", "keep running /babysit-prs"). Do NOT invoke for one-off tasks.
- schedule: Create, update, list, or run scheduled remote agents (routines) that execute on a cron schedule. - When the user wants to schedule a recurring remote agent, set up automated tasks, create a cron job for Claude Code, or manage their scheduled agents/routines. Also use when the user wants a one-time scheduled run ("run this once at 3pm", "remind me to check X tomorrow").
- claude-api: Build, debug, and optimize Claude API / Anthropic SDK apps. Apps built with this skill should include prompt caching. Also handles migrating existing Claude API code between Claude model versions (4.5 → 4.6, 4.6 → 4.7, retired-model replacements).
TRIGGER when: code imports `anthropic`/`@anthropic-ai/sdk`; user asks for the Claude API, Anthropic SDK, or Managed Agents; user adds/modifies/tunes a Claude feature (caching, thinking, compaction, tool use, batch, files, citations, memory) or model (Opus/Sonnet/Haiku) in a file; questions about prompt caching / cache hit rate in an Anthropic SDK project.
SKIP: file imports `openai`/other-provider SDK, filename like `*-openai.py`/`*-generic.py`, provider-neutral code, general programming/ML.
- run: Launch and drive this project's app to see a change working. Use when asked to run, start, or screenshot the app, or to confirm a change works in the real app (not just tests). First looks for a project skill that already covers launching the app; otherwise falls back to built-in patterns per project type (CLI, server, TUI, Electron, browser-driven, library).
- init: Initialize a new CLAUDE.md file with codebase documentation
- review: Review a pull request
- security-review: Complete a security review of the pending changes on the current branch

content.content:
1	# Radiacode App
2	
3	Kotlin Multiplatform repository for the Radiacode app — shared Kotlin business logic with **native UI per platform** (Compose for Android, SwiftUI for iOS).
4	
5	## Layout
6	
7	```
8	radiacode-app/
9	├── shared/         Kotlin Multiplatform library (commonMain, androidMain, iosMain)
10	├── androidApp/     Android app (Jetpack Compose, Material 3)
11	├── iosApp/         iOS project — RadiaCode app + Apple Watch app + widget extension + tests,
12	│                   generated from iosApp/project.yml via xcodegen
13	├── Makefile        `make ios` — regenerate & open the iOS Xcode project
14	└── gradle/         Version catalog and wrapper
15	```
16	
17	## Stack
18	
19	| Layer            | Tool / version            |
20	|------------------|---------------------------|
21	| Kotlin           | 2.3.21                    |
22	| Gradle           | 9.5.1                     |
23	| Android Gradle Plugin | 9.2.1 (KMP library plugin: `com.android.kotlin.multiplatform.library`) |
24	| Android compileSdk / minSdk / targetSdk | 36 / 26 / 36 |
25	| JDK              | 17+ (Java 21 from Android Studio JBR works) |
26	| Compose BOM      | 2026.05.01                |
27	| iOS deployment   | 17.0+                     |
28	| Xcode            | 26+                       |
29	| iOS consumption  | static `Shared.framework`, embedded via Xcode pre-build script (direct integration) |
30	
31	## Prerequisites
32	
33	- **Android Studio** Ladybug+ (ships with JDK 21) — provides Java for Gradle and the Android SDK
34	- **Xcode 26+** — for iOS builds
35	- **xcodegen** (for generating the iOS project from `iosApp/project.yml`):
36	  ```bash
37	  brew install xcodegen
38	  ```
39	- **Android SDK** at `~/Library/Android/sdk` (Android Studio installs it). Path stored in `local.properties`.
40	
41	If `java` isn't on your `PATH`, set:
42	```bash
43	export JAVA_HOME="/Applications/Android Studio.app/Contents/jbr/Contents/Home"
44	```
45	
46	## Run the Android app
47	
48	```bash
49	./gradlew :androidApp:installDebug    # installs on a running emulator/device
50	# or just build:
51	./gradlew :androidApp:assembleDebug
52	```
53	APK: `androidApp/build/outputs/apk/debug/androidApp-debug.apk`
54	
55	## Run the iOS app
56	
57	The Xcode project is **not committed** — it is generated from `iosApp/project.yml`. Generate and open it:
58	
59	```bash
60	make ios          # xcodegen generate + open iosApp/RadiaCode.xcodeproj
61	# or manually:
62	cd iosApp && xcodegen generate && open RadiaCode.xcodeproj
63	```
64	
65	Pick a simulator and hit **Run**. A pre-build script runs `./gradlew :shared:embedAndSignAppleFrameworkForXcode` on every build, so changes to the Kotlin `shared/` module are picked up automatically and the static `Shared.framework` is linked into the app.
66	
67	To build from the CLI:
68	```bash
69	xcodebuild -project iosApp/RadiaCode.xcodeproj \
70	           -scheme Radiacode \
71	           -destination 'generic/platform=iOS Simulator' \
72	           -configuration Debug build
73	```
74	> Drive the build with `-destination` (not `-sdk iphonesimulator`): the explicit SDK flag forces the embedded watchOS app onto the iOS simulator and collides with the iOS app product (`Multiple commands produce Radiacode.app`).
75	
76	## Regenerating the Xcode project
77	
78	`iosApp/RadiaCode.xcodeproj` is generated from `iosApp/project.yml` (and is git-ignored). After editing the YAML:
79	
80	```bash
81	cd iosApp && xcodegen generate
82	# or from the repo root:
83	make ios
84	```
85	
86	## Shared module
87	
88	- `commonMain/` — pure-Kotlin code, available to both platforms
89	- `androidMain/` — Android-only `actual` implementations (Android APIs, JVM libs)
90	- `iosMain/` — iOS-only `actual` implementations (UIKit, Foundation via Kotlin/Native interop)
91	
92	Hello-world example: `Greeting().greet()` returns `"Hello from Android 36!"` or `"Hello from iOS 18.0!"` depending on platform. The iOS app calls this once at launch (see `AppView.swift`) as a smoke check that the Kotlin↔Swift bridge links.
93	
94	## Notes
95	
96	- The iOS app links the **static** `Shared.framework` directly (no SPM / `Package.swift`); the `:shared:embedAndSignAppleFrameworkForXcode` pre-build script builds and embeds it for the active configuration/SDK. The framework search path is the default `$(BUILT_PRODUCTS_DIR)`.
97	- The shared module is a single Gradle module (`:shared`). Internal package layout (`com.radiacode.shared.protocol`, `.domain`, etc.) can be split into separate Gradle modules later if needed.
98	- `iosApp/` holds the full production iOS project (`RadiaCode/`, `RadiacodeWatch/`, `RadiacodeWidgetExtension/`, tests), vendored as a **snapshot** from the standalone `radiacode-ios` repo — the full git history stays in that repo, not in this monorepo. `RadiaCode.xcodeproj`, `Localizable.xcstrings` and `.sentryclirc` are generated/local and git-ignored (see `iosApp/.gitignore`).
99	- The Apple Watch target does **not** consume `:shared` — the shared module currently builds only iOS targets (`iosArm64`, `iosSimulatorArm64`, `iosX64`).
100	```
101

# Radiacode App

Kotlin Multiplatform repository for the Radiacode app — shared Kotlin business logic with **native UI per platform** (Compose for Android, SwiftUI for iOS).

## Layout

```
radiacode-app/
├── shared/         Kotlin Multiplatform library (commonMain, androidMain, iosMain)
├── androidApp/     Android app (Jetpack Compose, Material 3)
├── iosApp/         iOS project — RadiaCode app + Apple Watch app + widget extension + tests,
│                   generated from iosApp/project.yml via xcodegen
├── Makefile        `make ios` — regenerate & open the iOS Xcode project
└── gradle/         Version catalog and wrapper
```

## Stack

| Layer            | Tool / version            |
|------------------|---------------------------|
| Kotlin           | 2.3.21                    |
| Gradle           | 9.5.1                     |
| Android Gradle Plugin | 9.2.1 (KMP library plugin: `com.android.kotlin.multiplatform.library`) |
| Android compileSdk / minSdk / targetSdk | 36 / 26 / 36 |
| JDK              | 17+ (Java 21 from Android Studio JBR works) |
| Compose BOM      | 2026.05.01                |
| iOS deployment   | 17.0+                     |
| Xcode            | 26+                       |
| iOS consumption  | static `Shared.framework`, embedded via Xcode pre-build script (direct integration) |

## Prerequisites

- **Android Studio** Ladybug+ (ships with JDK 21) — provides Java for Gradle and the Android SDK
- **Xcode 26+** — for iOS builds
- **xcodegen** (for generating the iOS project from `iosApp/project.yml`):
  ```bash
  brew install xcodegen
  ```
- **Android SDK** at `~/Library/Android/sdk` (Android Studio installs it). Path stored in `local.properties`.

If `java` isn't on your `PATH`, set:
```bash
export JAVA_HOME="/Applications/Android Studio.app/Contents/jbr/Contents/Home"
```

## Run the Android app

```bash
./gradlew :androidApp:installDebug    # installs on a running emulator/device
# or just build:
./gradlew :androidApp:assembleDebug
```
APK: `androidApp/build/outputs/apk/debug/androidApp-debug.apk`

## Run the iOS app

The Xcode project is **not committed** — it is generated from `iosApp/project.yml`. Generate and open it:

```bash
make ios          # xcodegen generate + open iosApp/RadiaCode.xcodeproj
# or manually:
cd iosApp && xcodegen generate && open RadiaCode.xcodeproj
```

Pick a simulator and hit **Run**. A pre-build script runs `./gradlew :shared:embedAndSignAppleFrameworkForXcode` on every build, so changes to the Kotlin `shared/` module are picked up automatically and the static `Shared.framework` is linked into the app.

To build from the CLI:
```bash
xcodebuild -project iosApp/RadiaCode.xcodeproj \
           -scheme Radiacode \
           -destination 'generic/platform=iOS Simulator' \
           -configuration Debug build
```
> Drive the build with `-destination` (not `-sdk iphonesimulator`): the explicit SDK flag forces the embedded watchOS app onto the iOS simulator and collides with the iOS app product (`Multiple commands produce Radiacode.app`).

## Regenerating the Xcode project

`iosApp/RadiaCode.xcodeproj` is generated from `iosApp/project.yml` (and is git-ignored). After editing the YAML:

```bash
cd iosApp && xcodegen generate
# or from the repo root:
make ios
```

## Shared module

- `commonMain/` — pure-Kotlin code, available to both platforms
- `androidMain/` — Android-only `actual` implementations (Android APIs, JVM libs)
- `iosMain/` — iOS-only `actual` implementations (UIKit, Foundation via Kotlin/Native interop)

Hello-world example: `Greeting().greet()` returns `"Hello from Android 36!"` or `"Hello from iOS 18.0!"` depending on platform. The iOS app calls this once at launch (see `AppView.swift`) as a smoke check that the Kotlin↔Swift bridge links.

## Notes

- The iOS app links the **static** `Shared.framework` directly (no SPM / `Package.swift`); the `:shared:embedAndSignAppleFrameworkForXcode` pre-build script builds and embeds it for the active configuration/SDK. The framework search path is the default `$(BUILT_PRODUCTS_DIR)`.
- The shared module is a single Gradle module (`:shared`). Internal package layout (`com.radiacode.shared.protocol`, `.domain`, etc.) can be split into separate Gradle modules later if needed.
- `iosApp/` holds the full production iOS project (`RadiaCode/`, `RadiacodeWatch/`, `RadiacodeWidgetExtension/`, tests), vendored as a **snapshot** from the standalone `radiacode-ios` repo — the full git history stays in that repo, not in this monorepo. `RadiaCode.xcodeproj`, `Localizable.xcstrings` and `.sentryclirc` are generated/local and git-ignored (see `iosApp/.gitignore`).
- The Apple Watch target does **not** consume `:shared` — the shared module currently builds only iOS targets (`iosArm64`, `iosSimulatorArm64`, `iosX64`).
```

Подтвердил: файл — `iosApp/.sentryclirc`, внутри `[auth]` → `token=…`.

**Что такое `.sentryclirc`:** это конфиг утилиты `sentry-cli` (Sentry CLI). Хранит auth-токен (и обычно org/project), которым во время сборки iOS заливаются dSYM-символы в Sentry, чтобы крэши в проде были читаемыми (с именами функций/строками). Токен — секрет, поэтому файл обязан быть в `.gitignore`. Сейчас он лежит как `iosApp/.sentryclirc`.

И спасибо, что спросил — в моём черновике была опечатка `.sentriclirc` (через `i`). С ней правило **не сработало бы** и файл с токеном попал бы в репо. Исправляю на `.sentryclirc`.

Вношу обе правки в предложение. Меняются только два нижних блока:

```gitignore

.claude/

toolUseResult.originalFile:
# Radiacode App

Kotlin Multiplatform repository for the Radiacode app — shared Kotlin business logic with **native UI per platform** (Compose for Android, SwiftUI for iOS).

## Layout

```
radiacode-app/
├── shared/         Kotlin Multiplatform library (commonMain, androidMain, iosMain)
├── androidApp/     Android app (Jetpack Compose, Material 3)
├── iosApp/         iOS project — RadiaCode app + Apple Watch app + widget extension + tests,
│                   generated from iosApp/project.yml via xcodegen
├── Makefile        `make ios` — regenerate & open the iOS Xcode project
└── gradle/         Version catalog and wrapper
```

## Stack

| Layer            | Tool / version            |
|------------------|---------------------------|
| Kotlin           | 2.3.21                    |
| Gradle           | 9.5.1                     |
| Android Gradle Plugin | 9.2.1 (KMP library plugin: `com.android.kotlin.multiplatform.library`) |
| Android compileSdk / minSdk / targetSdk | 36 / 26 / 36 |
| JDK              | 17+ (Java 21 from Android Studio JBR works) |
| Compose BOM      | 2026.05.01                |
| iOS deployment   | 17.0+                     |
| Xcode            | 26+                       |
| iOS consumption  | static `Shared.framework`, embedded via Xcode pre-build script (direct integration) |

## Prerequisites

- **Android Studio** Ladybug+ (ships with JDK 21) — provides Java for Gradle and the Android SDK
- **Xcode 26+** — for iOS builds
- **xcodegen** (for generating the iOS project from `iosApp/project.yml`):
  ```bash
  brew install xcodegen
  ```
- **Android SDK** at `~/Library/Android/sdk` (Android Studio installs it). Path stored in `local.properties`.

If `java` isn't on your `PATH`, set:
```bash
export JAVA_HOME="/Applications/Android Studio.app/Contents/jbr/Contents/Home"
```

## Run the Android app

```bash
./gradlew :androidApp:installDebug    # installs on a running emulator/device
# or just build:
./gradlew :androidApp:assembleDebug
```
APK: `androidApp/build/outputs/apk/debug/androidApp-debug.apk`

## Run the iOS app

The Xcode project is **not committed** — it is generated from `iosApp/project.yml`. Generate and open it:

```bash
make ios          # xcodegen generate + open iosApp/RadiaCode.xcodeproj
# or manually:
cd iosApp && xcodegen generate && open RadiaCode.xcodeproj
```

Pick a simulator and hit **Run**. A pre-build script runs `./gradlew :shared:embedAndSignAppleFrameworkForXcode` on every build, so changes to the Kotlin `shared/` module are picked up automatically and the static `Shared.framework` is linked into the app.

To build from the CLI:
```bash
xcodebuild -project iosApp/RadiaCode.xcodeproj \
           -scheme Radiacode \
           -destination 'generic/platform=iOS Simulator' \
           -configuration Debug build
```
> Drive the build with `-destination` (not `-sdk iphonesimulator`): the explicit SDK flag forces the embedded watchOS app onto the iOS simulator and collides with the iOS app product (`Multiple commands produce Radiacode.app`).

## Regenerating the Xcode project

`iosApp/RadiaCode.xcodeproj` is generated from `iosApp/project.yml` (and is git-ignored). After editing the YAML:

```bash
cd iosApp && xcodegen generate
# or from the repo root:
make ios
```

## Shared module

- `commonMain/` — pure-Kotlin code, available to both platforms
- `androidMain/` — Android-only `actual` implementations (Android APIs, JVM libs)
- `iosMain/` — iOS-only `actual` implementations (UIKit, Foundation via Kotlin/Native interop)

Hello-world example: `Greeting().greet()` returns `"Hello from Android 36!"` or `"Hello from iOS 18.0!"` depending on platform. The iOS app calls this once at launch (see `AppView.swift`) as a smoke check that the Kotlin↔Swift bridge links.

## Notes

- The iOS app links the **static** `Shared.framework` directly (no SPM / `Package.swift`); the `:shared:embedAndSignAppleFrameworkForXcode` pre-build script builds and embeds it for the active configuration/SDK. The framework search path is the default `$(BUILT_PRODUCTS_DIR)`.
- The shared module is a single Gradle module (`:shared`). Internal package layout (`com.radiacode.shared.protocol`, `.domain`, etc.) can be split into separate Gradle modules later if needed.

content.text:
Готово. Один `.gitignore` в корне, iOS-правила влиты, поведение сохранено.

**Что сделано:**
- [.gitignore](.gitignore) — переписан: объединение обоих файлов без дублей, секция `# Xcode / iOS` + `# Swift Package Manager` + `# iOS packaging` + `# Fastlane`, `.sentryclirc` (исправлена опечатка), `.claude/` целиком.
- `iosApp/.gitignore` — удалён.
- [README.md:98](README.md:98) — ссылка `(see iosApp/.gitignore)` → `(see the root .gitignore)`.

**Проверка (read-only):**
- Все критичные файлы по-прежнему игнорируются новым корневым правилом: `iosApp/.sentryclirc` (токен), `iosApp/RadiaCode.xcodeproj`, `Localizable.xcstrings`, `.claude/`. ✓
- Ни один отслеживаемый файл не попал под игнор. ✓
- Новых «вылезших» незаигноренных файлов нет — в рабочем дереве ровно три моих изменения:

```
 M .gitignore
 M README.md
 D iosApp/.gitignore
```

Изменения **не закоммичены** — git-операций без твоей команды не делаю. Скажешь — закоммичу (по конвенции: `RMP-35 [Core] Настройка .gitignore`) или открою PR.

Я тронул README всего в одном месте — на [строке 98](README.md:98) поменял ровно одно слово в скобках:

- было: `… git-ignored (see iosApp/.gitignore).`
- стало: `… git-ignored (see the root .gitignore).`

Больше ничего не добавлял и не растягивал — размер README не менялся, он и до меня был ~100 строк.

Похоже, правка README для задачи про `.gitignore` тебе кажется лишней. Если так — откачу её, и RMP-35 останется чисто про `.gitignore` (тогда в README сохранится упоминание уже удалённого `iosApp/.gitignore` — это будет немного устаревшей строкой, но вне рамок задачи).

Скажи, что предпочитаешь:
- **откатить мою правку README** (README не трогаем вообще);
- **оставить как есть** (одно слово);
- или ты про то, что **сам README великоват** и хочешь его ужать — это отдельная история, могу заняться отдельно.

Что делаем?

Понял — делаю досканальный аудит итогового `.gitignore`: проверю, что ничего не потеряно при слиянии, нет дублей, все генерируемые/секретные пути по-прежнему игнорируются, исходники — нет, и нет регрессий из-за смены анкеринга. Это read-only проверки (правок и коммитов не делаю).

content.text:
Досканальный аудит готов. Итог: **слияние корректное, потерь нет, секреты закрыты — нашёл одну реальную регрессию.**

**Чисто:**
- Дублей значащих строк нет.
- Все старые правила покрыты. Три не перенесены буквально, но эквивалентны/перекрыты:
  - `xcuserdata` → `xcuserdata/` (это всегда папка),
  - `.swiftpm` → `.swiftpm/` (папка),
  - `*/build/*` → перекрыто более широким `build/`.
- Всё генерируемое/секретное игнорируется: `build/` (вкл. `androidApp/build`, `shared/build`), `.gradle`, `.idea`, `.kotlin`, `local.properties`, `iosApp/RadiaCode.xcodeproj`, **`iosApp/.sentryclirc`** (токен), `Localizable.xcstrings`, `.claude/`.
- Исходники/конфиги НЕ задеты: `project.yml`, `*.build.gradle.kts`, `settings.gradle.kts`, `gradle.properties`, `proguard-rules.pro`, `gradle-wrapper.jar`, `.swiftlint.yml`, скрипты, доки — всё трекается.
- Ни один **отслеживаемый** файл не попал под игнор.

**Найдена 1 регрессия — анкеринг `/.previous-build`:**
В старом `iosApp/.gitignore` строка `/.previous-build` была относительна папки `iosApp/`, т.е. игнорировала `iosApp/.previous-build` (кеш-файл xcodegen рядом с `project.yml`). После переноса в корень ведущий `/` стал означать корень репо — теперь `iosApp/.previous-build` **не игнорируется**, а игнорируется несуществующий `<repo>/.previous-build`.

Фикс — убрать ведущий слэш, чтобы правило ловило файл на любой глубине:

```diff
- /.previous-build
+ .previous-build
```

Остальные «расширения охвата» (`*.entitlements`, `Packages/`, `**/Generated/`, `fastlane/*` теперь действуют на весь монорепо, а не только `iosApp/`) — это осознанное следствие плоского единого файла, которое ты уже одобрил; на текущую структуру репо влияния нет (такие файлы есть только под `iosApp/`).

Внести фикс `/.previous-build` → `.previous-build`? Других правок не предлагаю.

Принято: «окей» — вношу фикс `/.previous-build` → `.previous-build`, и делаю PR. Сначала правка + соберу факты по remote/base-ветке (read-only), чтобы PR ушёл правильно.

Замечу: по правилам проекта (CLAUDE.md) — один коммит, сообщение `RMP-35 [Core] Настройка .gitignore`, без упоминания AI/Claude где-либо (ни co-author, ни футер в PR).

Диагноз понятен и важный: **коммит/PR #6 содержит `.gitignore` БЕЗ фикса** — в `HEAD` осталось `/.previous-build` (стр. 47), а одобренный тобой `.previous-build` висит незакоммиченным в рабочем дереве. Тот сбой `git add` на удалённом пути привёл к тому, что застейджилась более ранняя версия файла.

Исправляю так, чтобы осталось **по правилу проекта — один коммит**: добавляю фикс в тот же коммит через `--amend` и обновляю ветку (`--force-with-lease`). PR #6 подтянется сам. Это в рамках того, что ты уже одобрил (фикс + PR).
